text.title: Agent-Assisted Pairing Workflow
text.summary: Rules and gotchas for using an LLM agent as a pair programming participant.

rule.statement: Declare the pairing style explicitly at session start; mixed-style pairing degrades to "agent does everything".
rule.rationale: Without a stated style constraint, the agent defaults to autonomous execution and the pair dynamic collapses.

rule.statement: Maintain a session journal (.pair-journal.md) with the goal in one sentence; re-read on every compaction to preserve early decisions.
rule.rationale: Agent context resets across compaction; the journal is the shared memory that survives.

rule.statement: In strong-style mode, remove Edit/Write tools from the agent's allowed set; output only text instructions or snippets of at most 6 lines.
rule.rationale: Without a hard tool constraint the agent will quietly use Edit, violating the style contract.

section.title: Subagent Roles

list.item: pair-navigator (Sonnet): strategic-level partner; asks "what about empty input?", names patterns, spots risks.
tdd-pingpong: alternates roles; tracks whose turn it is in a session-state file.
tour-guide: read-only repo narrator; drives Glob/Read/Grep, explains architecture and history.
simplify (built-in skill): periodic "step back, simplify" pass at end of session.

section.reference: System prompt for strong-style agent pairing.
TDD ping-pong entry prompt with turn-tracking rules.

antipattern.description: Sycophancy: agent agrees with every design decision without pushback.
antipattern.reason: Agents lack the social authority of a senior engineer; periodically run a hostile-review subagent as counterweight.

antipattern.description: Scope creep: agent finds adjacent issues and expands the task.
antipattern.reason: Pin scope in the journal; reject out-of-scope edits in the turn-end check.

antipattern.description: Ping-pong cheating: agent writes test AND production code in the same turn.
antipattern.reason: Enforce turn-state in the journal; abort when violated.

antipattern.description: Privacy: agent reads .env, secrets, customer data while exploring.
antipattern.reason: Pre-scope allowed paths; use a password-scrubber agent or exclude sensitive files from context.

rule.statement: Force the agent to ask "should I?" before any non-trivial change; sycophantic auto-edit kills the pair dynamic.
rule.rationale: The value of a navigator is the deliberate pause before action, not speed.

text.reference: Session journal script that survives agent compaction.
